title: Echeveria rosei
intro: Echeveria rosei, also known as Rose Echeveria, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family, characterized by its beautiful rosy-pink leaves and compact growth habit. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Echeveria rosei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Echeveria rosei is used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic potential.
taxonomy: Echeveria rosei is a species of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. This family includes various other succulent genera such as Crassula, Sedum, and Kalanchoe. The genus Echeveria itself contains around 150 species of succulents, which are primarily native to the Americas. Echeveria rosei is a type of rosette-forming succulent, characterized by its compact growth habit and attractive foliage. Within the Crassulaceae family, Echeveria rosei is part of the subfamily Sempervivoideae, which also includes other succulent genera.

side_effects: Echeveria rosei is a type of succulent, and while there is limited research on its potential side effects, some people may experience minor issues if they ingest or come into contact with its sap.
